source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane, Other Lifting - Thunder Horse Platform

actions_taken: Crew performed troubleshooting over seven days to restore power
Emergency response team detected carbon monoxide in battery room
Personnel evacuated 265 out of 298 people from facility
Emergency Generator started to maintain power
VMS cable physically removed to prevent repeated abandon platform sequences
Auxiliary Generators started to restore full power
UPS-B system troubleshooting completed and tested
event.summary: On September 18, 2017, at 1100 hours, the Thunder Horse Platform (MC 778-A) experienced a complete loss of electrical power including emergency power. The incident was triggered by a UPS-B inverter failure on September 17, followed by a static bypass switch opening due to frequency synchronization tolerance mismatch between the Emergency Generator and Turbine Generators. The facility's Vessel Management System incorrectly maintained an abandon platform alarm state when rebooted, causing repeated power losses. A carbon monoxide gas release was detected in the UPS-B battery room. The incident resulted in the evacuation of 265 personnel and took approximately 24 hours to restore emergency power and several days to fully restore facility operations.
recommendations: Create or revise a 'Black Start' procedure that includes complete loss of power to the VMS and/or PLC
Consult with OEM to evaluate and make appropriate recommendations for UPS protective features
Review the project engineering handover process for critical electrical systems to ensure personnel are properly trained to operate new equipment
Create or review a platform evacuation procedure specifically prompted by a facility blackout
Verify all necessary SEMS documentation is up-to-date and accessible in hard-copy format
Review 30 CFR 250.842 regarding changes to Facilities Electrical One-line drawing constituting a Facility Safety System Permit Review
Review the performance of critical ventilation systems relative to their basis of design and develop a strategy to address findings

consequences.name: Loss of Vessel Management System Control
Repeated Power Cycling and System Instability
Carbon Monoxide Gas Release in Battery Room

controls.name: UPS-B System with Inverter and Static Bypass Mode
controls.barrier_role: Provide backup power to Emergency Motor Control Centers
controls.lod_basis: Primary backup power system for critical facility loads

supporting_text: On September 17, 2017, at 2000 hours, the MC 778-A (Thunder Horse) UPS-B inverter failed but normal power continued to supply the Emergency Motor Control Center(s) (EMCC) when the UPS switched to Static Bypass Mode.
The following morning on September 18, 2017, at approximately 0700 hours, a BP electrician discovered the alarms on UPS-B during normal rounds.

controls.name: Fixed Hydrogen Gas Detectors in Battery Room
controls.barrier_role: Detect abnormal hydrogen concentrations in battery room
controls.lod_basis: Early detection of gas generation from battery system

supporting_text: There are three fixed hydrogen gas detectors in the battery room and none of them were detecting an abnormal amount of hydrogen.

controls.name: Portable Gas Detection Equipment (Altair 4x and 5x IR Detectors)
controls.barrier_role: Manual detection of carbon monoxide and hydrogen in battery room
controls.lod_basis: Secondary detection method when fixed detectors fail

supporting_text: After the electrician left the space briefly, he returned with a hand-held Altair 4x gas infrared (IR) detector and detected carbon monoxide inside battery room D2311

The SL arrived at the UPS-B room at approximately 0750 hours with a calibrated MSA Altair 5x IR Meter equipped with a wand, and with the battery room's door slightly cracked open, started taking readings at the door by inserting the gas detector probe through the crack at the breathing zone.

controls.name: Battery Room Ventilation System
controls.barrier_role: Remove gases generated by battery system to maintain safe atmosphere
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent gas accumulation in battery room

supporting_text: The IT inspected the airflow from the exterior to confirm the airflow to the battery room. The volume of air was not sufficient for the battery room's design and believed the low flow might not be enough to properly ventilate the battery room.

The crew checked the intake with portable gas detectors and found no issues with that area based on the location of the supply vents. The Electrician team checked multiple vents and was able to identify the exhaust vent location for the D2311 battery room that is located under the heliport.

controls.name: Emergency Response Team (ERT) Coordination and Decision Making
controls.barrier_role: Coordinate response to power loss and gas release incidents
controls.lod_basis: Organizational response to mitigate consequences of power failure

supporting_text: The ERT discussed the aforementioned CO readings and retested to confirm the initial readings. For safety reasons, the SL decided to keep the door to the battery room closed and not allow anyone in until the ERT could figure out what was going on.
The crew then discussed the ventilation supply and exhaust for the UPS-B room and after proceeded to the upper deck on the starboard side where the exhaust stacks are located.

controls.name: General Alarm and Personnel Muster Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Alert personnel and organize evacuation from facility
controls.lod_basis: Precautionary measure to ensure personnel safety during blackout

supporting_text: At that point, the Offshore Instillation Manager (OIM) decided to make an announcement to muster all personnel onboard since the UPS room was next to the living quarters where personnel were sleeping as a precautionary measure. The OIM instructed the Marine crew to activate the general alarm. When the general alarm sounded, personnel mustered in a safe fashion to their designated muster areas. All personnel were accounted for and were informed of the situation onboard the facility.

controls.name: Well Barriers (Blind Shear Rams and Additional Barriers)
controls.barrier_role: Isolate wells during power loss to prevent uncontrolled flow
controls.lod_basis: Primary well isolation during loss of power to control systems

supporting_text: Before disconnecting power supply to UPS-B batteries, the Wells Crew that was conducting well work closed and locked the blind shear rams in addition to two other barriers that were already in place.

controls.name: Emergency Generator (EG) System
controls.barrier_role: Provide backup power when main turbine generators are lost
controls.lod_basis: Secondary power source to maintain critical facility functions

supporting_text: Since UPS-B could not supply battery power due to its inverter failure, the ERT decided to preemptively start the Emergency Generator (EG) so that power would be maintained if the main power Turbine Generators (TG) were lost.
In the process of syncing the frequency of power from the EG with the TGs, UPS-B's static bypass switch opened due to the frequencies being out of tolerance by just over one percent.

controls.name: Subsurface Controlled Surface Safety Valves (SCSSVs), Shutdown Valves (SDVs), and Boarding Shutdown Valves (BSDVs)
controls.barrier_role: Automatically close wells when Emergency Shutdown (ESD) is activated
controls.lod_basis: Automatic well isolation per Deepwater Operation Plan

supporting_text: All the Subsurface Controlled Surface Safety Valves (SCSSVs), Shutdown Valves (SDVs), and Boarding Shutdown Valves (BSDVs) closed per the approved Deepwater Operation Plan (DWOP) when the ESD on the facility was activated.

controls.name: Vessel Management System (VMS) with Abandon Platform Sequence
controls.barrier_role: Automatically shut down main power and initiate safe shutdown sequence
controls.lod_basis: Automated response to loss of critical power

supporting_text: When the VMS rebooted, an abandon platform sequence was initiated which shut down all main power TGs, tripped the EG to emergency bus breaker, and caused the production process to fail in a safe state.

It was unknown at the time that the VMS was still initiating an abandon platform sequence. This cycle occurred two more times while crew was troubleshooting the blackout. Ultimately personnel were able to identify that the VMS was still initiating an abandon platform signal when rebooted which opened the breaker between the EG and the emergency bus.
Personnel physically removed the VMS cable to the breaker that was causing it to open until they could reinstate power and clear the abandon platform signal.

controls.name: UPS Logic Settings and Frequency Synchronization Tolerance
controls.barrier_role: Protect UPS static switch from frequency mismatch during power synchronization

supporting_text: BP was unaware that the UPS logic from the manufacturer had a syncing tolerance of one percent to protect the equipment's static switch as opposed to BP's normal set point of five percent.
Lack of a formal mechanism to specify logic settings in the UPS system for use in this setup

controls.name: Project Engineering Handover and Documentation
controls.barrier_role: Ensure proper communication and training on new UPS equipment specifications
controls.lod_basis: Organizational control to prevent knowledge gaps in critical system operation

supporting_text: Inadequate communication of the UPS Replacement Project documentation
Review the project engineering handover process for critical electrical systems to ensure personnel are properly trained to operate new equipment.

controls.name: Auxiliary Generators (AG) System
controls.barrier_role: Provide backup power when Emergency Generator is unavailable
controls.lod_basis: Tertiary power source for facility habitability and operations

supporting_text: The essential crew began preparation to start the Auxiliary Generators (AG), but before starting the AGs, smoke was detected in the UPS-A room at approximately 2140 hours.
They were successful in restoring full power via the EG and AGs.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
